author: @Staedford
text: The boat propeller performs badly because it is the wrong orientation, it was trying to turn anticlockwise when the vortex was clockwise.  So you can't just flip it over, you need to make or use a different (handed) propeller.

author: @matteoricci9129
text: Put more "petals" on the propeller maybe it helps

author: @spanners7343
text: The reason for the difference in the efficiency of the turbines is that your design is for low torque/high rpm which is the same as the fan motor, the propeller design is a high torque/low rpm design. If you used a high torque/low rpm motor for the generator you should find the propeller blade would be more efficient.

author: @PhilWaud
text: Great video, thank you. Do you think that using a smaller pipe or larger prop blade would increase output? Clearly the vortex is doing its job and throwing the water (and therefore the kinetic energy) to the outside of the pipe and its missing the blade?

author: @Andy-df5fj
text: The Coriolis effect is negligible on small scales.  What dictates the vortex direction is the direction from which the water is dumped.

replies.author: @Andy-df5fj
replies.text:
@ThinkingandTinkering 
When it is the only force, even the negligible force wins.  If you dump that bucket from the other direction, it will spin the other way.

author: @kilokilos
text: With a high-pressure system, air gets forced outward and the Earth's eastward spin creates a clockwise flow. In the Southern Hemisphere, the opposite takes place: Wind around low-pressure systems circles clockwise while wind around high-pressure systems circles counterclockwise, coliorus effect. How would a Tesla turbine work in water?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: in this situation very badly - you really need pressure to make a Tesla turbine work well mate

author: @chrisroberts9791
text: When you flipped the propeller over, it looked like the blade angles do not reverse 180 degrees relative to the spinning vortex. The blades still split the water stream. Or did I miss something completely?

replies.author: @jclowe735
replies.text:
No you didn't miss anything just look at a boar with two or more engines on it and you may notice the propellers spin in different rotations.

One propeller will spin clockwise and the other propeller will spin counterclockwise on a boat with two or more engines.

replies.author: @hommerdalor6301
replies.text: 🤣😂 Same joke as The vortex will turn the other way in Australia.

replies.author: @hanslepoeter5167
replies.text: It's exactly the same if you flip it over. So that's a bit poorly explained. It's what is called a right hand propeller. Flipping it over doesn't change that. What is needed is a left hand propeller. But the point Robert is making is clear is is it not.

replies.author: @hanslepoeter5167
replies.text: @jclowe735 With 2 engines. That is correct and intended to eliminate what is called  "propeller walk"  or "wheel effect". The tendency for a boat to head a bit more to the left for a right hand propeller and a bit more to the right for a left hand propeller. Like the propeller has grip on the bottom like a wheel.

replies.author: @jclowe735
replies.text: @hanslepoeter5167 you get what I was saying and what I meant with what I said but you added to my point with more information than what I had.
